body_txt: A teenager growing up in a small rural town wants to get away from it all, to the big city, or abroad, anywhere but the boring old Small Town. Characters who come from this background, such as the Country Mouse and Farm Boy , generally Jump at the Call . If they don't, expect the call to come looking for them anyway . Often, this overlaps with Nothing Exciting Ever Happens Here and Hated Hometown , and is seen in stories set in Dying Towns , perhaps ones with Small Town Rivalry . How sympathetic the character suffering from Small Town Boredom is depends on a per-work basis. If they are sick of homely, pastoral, pleasant Arcadia , it usually leads to An Aesop about Home Sweet Home and appreciating what you've got, a common subtype being that of leaving the Close-Knit Community and finding out that Apathetic Citizens are much worse. If they want to get away from a suffocating Small Town, Big Hell , however, they are usually viewed (and treated) with much more respect - doubly so if they are LGBTQIA+ or otherwise struggling to conform to the narrow-minded townsfolk. If a specific town is named, expect it to be Toledo, Ohio or Cleveland . When this happens in a musical, expect a "Somewhere" Song or a Wanderlust Song . Related to Grass Is Greener , which is about someone in bad conditions dreaming of going to a better place. Contrast the inverse trope, Big Town Boredom , when a character living in a bigger city or suburb wants to move to a small town or just anywhere that's generally smaller and more rural, or From New York to Nowhere .
